data.title: Trump says open to tariff negotiations, contradicting White House aides
data.description:
U.S. President Donald Trump said he would be open to negotiating with other countries for “phenomenal” offerings, contradicting White House aides who insist the sweeping tariffs are non-negotiable.

“If somebody said that we’re going to give you something that’s so phenomenal, as long as they’re giving us something that’s good,” he told reporters aboard Air Force One on Thursday.

His comments come after several White House officials insisted that the newly announced reciprocal tariffs were not negotiable. Top Trump trade advisor Peter Navarro told CNBC the tariffs are “not a negotiation.”

The President’s tariff plans sent global stock markets plunging and wiped out roughly $2 trillion in the S&P 500 on Thursday. Wall Street experienced its worst session since the 2020 pandemic period.

New U.S. tariffs on auto imports also took effect overnight, prompting Stellantis (STLA) to pause production at its plants in Canada and Mexico.

Market-tracking ETFs include:  (SPY), (QQQ), (DIA), (VOO), (IVV), (DOG) (SQQQ), (RSP), (UPRO), (SH), and (QLD).

Dear readers: We recognize that politics often intersects with the financial news of the day, so we invite you to click here to join the separate political discussion.

data.title: Nissan to halt US orders for SUVs built in Mexico after Trump's tariffs shock
data.description:
• Nissan Motor (OTCPK:NSANF) (OTCPK:NSANY) has stopped taking new U.S. orders for its Mexican-built Infiniti SUVs due to tariffs imposed by the Trump administration.
  • The move affects its joint venture with Mercedes-Benz (OTCPK:MBGYY) (OTCPK:MBGAF). 
  • Nissan Motor (OTCPK:NSANF) (OTCPK:NSANY) said it will maintain its Tennessee plant's Rogue production, after announcing in January it would end one of the two shifts this month.
  • However, it will stop accepting additional U.S. orders for the Infiniti QX50 and QX55 models produced at the COMPAS plant in Mexico, which serves multiple markets including the Middle East and Canada.
  • Trump's 25% global car and truck tariffs took effect on Thursday.

data.title: Friday's Economic Calendar
data.description:
8:30 AM Employment Situation

The most closely watched of all economic indicators, the employment situation is a set of monthly labor market indicators based on two separate reports: the establishment survey which tracks 650,000 worksites and offers the nonfarm payroll and the household survey which interviews 60,000 households and generates the unemployment rate.

Payrolls are seen up by a somewhat muted 131,000 with the jobless rate rising to 4.2 percent from 4.1 percent in February. Private payrolls are expected up 115,000.

11:25 AM Fed's Powell Speech

Federal Reserve Chairman Jerome Powell will speak on the economic outlook before the Society for Advancing Business Editing and Writing (SABEW) Annual Conference.

12:00 PM Fed's Barr Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Michael Barr will speak on"Artificial Intelligence and Banking" before the Federal Reserve Bank of San Francisco, San Francisco State University, and University of California Santa Cruz 2025 Fintech Conference.

12:45 PM Fed's Waller Speech

Federal Reserve Board Governor Christopher Waller will participate in"Payments" discussion before the 2025 New York Fed Innovation Conference.

1:00 PM Baker Hughes Rig Count

The Baker Hughes North American rig count tracks weekly changes in the number of active operating oil & gas rigs. Used for drilling wellbores for wells that may eventually produce oil or gas, active rigs are essential for the exploration and development of oil and gas fields.

data.title: Guess? outlines fiscal 2026 revenue growth of up to 6.2% amid strategic initiatives
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Guess?, Inc. (GES) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Carlos Alberini highlighted the completion of several strategic initiatives, including the acquisition of rag & bone, the launch of Guess Jeans targeting a younger demographic, and expanding the Guess brand in India with 22 new stores. He emphasized the importance of leveraging Guess’s operating platform across multiple brands.
  • The company reported Q4 revenues of $932 million, a 5% increase in US dollars, driven by the acquisition of rag & bone and growth in the core Guess business. Adjusted EPS for the quarter was $1.48.
  • Alberini announced key leadership appointments, including Alberto Toni as Chief Financial Officer starting in June and Lorenzo Maria Di Vecchio as General Counsel.
  • CFO Dennis Secor detailed that European wholesale grew mid-single digits despite currency headwinds, with the fall-winter collection order book up 7%. Licensing revenues grew 18%, driven by strong performance in footwear, fragrances, and handbags.

Outlook

  • Fiscal 2026 revenue growth is projected to range between 3.9% and 6.2%, supported by the full-year contribution of rag & bone and expansion in the Middle East through a joint venture with the Chalhoub Group.
  • Adjusted operating margin for the year is expected to be between 4.5% and 5.4%, with adjusted EPS guidance ranging from $1.32 to $1.76.
  • First quarter fiscal 2026 revenue growth is anticipated between 5.8% and 7.5%, with an adjusted loss per share projected between ($0.74) and ($0.65).

Financial Results

  • Q4 gross margin was reported at 44.1%, impacted by higher markdown pressure and unfavorable currency effects. Adjusted SG&A expenses rose 11% due to increased marketing investments and the addition of rag & bone.
  • For the fiscal year, revenues grew 8% in US dollars to $3 billion, with adjusted EPS reaching $1.96.
  • Inventory levels increased 21% year-over-year, primarily due to the acquisition of rag & bone and accelerated deliveries to mitigate supply chain disruptions.

Q&A

  • Mauricio Serna, UBS: Asked about growth drivers for rag & bone in FY 2026. CEO Alberini cited strong e-commerce performance, plans for new store openings, and expanding wholesale and licensing categories as key contributors.
  • Eric Beder, Small Cap Consumer Research: Inquired about the opportunity for Guess Jeans in the US and Europe. Alberini noted strong wholesale performance in Europe and plans to strengthen the US wholesale team, along with new store openings in Tokyo and West Hollywood.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts raised concerns about retail performance in the Americas and Asia. Queries focused on strategies to address pricing sensitivities and improve customer engagement.
  • Management expressed confidence in long-term growth opportunities, emphasizing strategic initiatives and investments in marketing. CEO Alberini stated, "We are excited about our growth opportunities... and remain fully committed to maximizing our potential."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a similar tone of cautious optimism, while analysts appeared more focused on addressing regional challenges.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Q4 revenue growth of 5% contrasts with 13% growth in Q3, influenced by currency headwinds and a decline in Americas retail performance.
  • Management tone remained consistent, emphasizing strategic initiatives and long-term growth, while addressing current challenges such as pricing and traffic declines.
  • Analysts continued to focus on the impact of external factors, including inflation and tariffs, on the company’s financial performance.

Risks and Concerns

  • Management highlighted challenges in the Americas retail segment, with a 14% decline in constant-currency comps attributed to traffic headwinds and conversion declines.
  • The Asia segment reported revenue declines, particularly in South Korea and China, due to ongoing traffic challenges.
  • Analysts expressed concerns about potential tariff impacts and the company’s ability to navigate pricing sensitivities in competitive markets.

Final Takeaway

Guess?, Inc. has outlined a strategic roadmap for fiscal 2026, targeting up to 6.2% revenue growth and emphasizing the expansion of its brand portfolio, particularly through rag & bone and Guess Jeans. While challenges persist in the Americas and Asia retail markets, management’s focus on marketing, pricing strategies, and operational efficiencies demonstrates a commitment to long-term growth and shareholder value.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Simulations plus reaffirms FY 2025 guidance with 28%-33% revenue growth target
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Simulations Plus (SLP) Q2 2025

Management View

  • CEO Shawn O’Connor highlighted a 23% year-over-year revenue increase, with 5% organic growth, driven by the Adaptive Learning & Insights (ALI) and Medical Communication (MC) business units. Adjusted EBITDA was $6.6 million, representing 29% of revenue.

  • Renewals for software products are maintaining historical levels despite funding challenges. QSP software revenue surged by 89%, primarily due to a model license for atopic dermatitis. CHEM business unit software revenue increased 8%, and there were 10 new customers alongside seven upsells.

  • Services revenue rose 34%, influenced by strong bookings in the CPP and MC units, although some project initiations have been delayed to the second half of the year. The services backlog increased to $20.4 million, up 18% sequentially.

  • CFO Will Frederick noted that software revenue comprised 60% of total revenue at $22.4 million, while services accounted for 40%. Gross margins declined to 59%, impacted by higher amortization expenses and increased service-related costs.

Outlook

  • FY 2025 guidance is reaffirmed, projecting total revenue between $90 million and $93 million, representing 28%-33% growth. Adjusted EBITDA margins are expected between 31%-33%, and adjusted diluted EPS between $1.07 and $1.20.

  • Management anticipates Q3 revenue to account for approximately 25% of FY 2025, with year-over-year growth of 21%-25%. A sequential revenue increase is forecasted for Q4.

  • O’Connor emphasized priorities including ramping up the ALI and MC business units, expanding cross-selling opportunities, and improving EBITDA margins to historical levels of 35%-40%.

Financial Results

  • Software revenue grew 16% year-over-year, with key contributions from GastroPlus (46%), MonolixSuite (23%), and ADMET Predictor (17%). ALI and MC software revenue were $0.9 million and $0.1 million, respectively.

  • Services revenue growth was led by CPP at 39% and MC at 25%. QSP and PBPK services contributed 19% and 17%, respectively. The services backlog grew to $20.4 million, reflecting an 18% increase from the prior quarter.

  • Gross margin for software was 81%, while services gross margin declined to 25%. The overall gross margin contraction was attributed to increased costs, including $0.8 million of amortization related to the Pro-ficiency acquisition.

Q&A

  • David Larsen, BTIG: Questioned software organic revenue growth and renewal rate decline. CFO Frederick clarified that organic software growth was 8%, with a renewal rate of 90%, affected by the timing of a significant account renewal.

  • Scott Schoenhaus, KeyBanc Capital Markets: Inquired about services revenue ramp and cross-selling opportunities. CEO O’Connor indicated robust pipeline activity, with a notable $5 million contract in the MC unit contributing to future revenue.

  • Christine Rains, William Blair: Asked about services backlog realization and federal funding risks. O’Connor noted that 90% of the backlog is realizable within 12 months and that federal funding reductions pose minimal risk.

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts expressed cautious optimism, focusing on challenges in project delays and budget constraints but acknowledging strong software performance and backlog growth. Concerns about services ramp timing were prominent.

  • Management demonstrated confidence in achieving FY 2025 guidance, emphasizing resilience in their software business and strategic priorities for expansion. Prepared remarks were positive, with a slightly defensive tone during Q&A regarding delays and macroeconomic pressures.

  • Compared to the previous quarter, management maintained a consistent optimism, while analysts showed increased scrutiny on services execution and backlog realization.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Revenue growth decelerated slightly from 31% in Q1 to 23% in Q2, with software organic growth also declining from 18% to 8%.

  • Services backlog increased from $17.3 million to $20.4 million, reflecting strong bookings despite delays in project starts.

  • Management reaffirmed FY 2025 guidance, maintaining confidence despite macroeconomic challenges, aligning with their prior outlook.

Risks and Concerns

  • Delays in services project initiations could potentially affect revenue timing. Management indicated that these delays are expected to push revenues to the latter half of the fiscal year.

  • Analysts raised concerns about the cautious spending environment among clients, particularly in the services segment.

  • Federal funding cuts and potential tariff impacts were noted as risks, though management minimized their direct exposure.

Final Takeaway

Simulations Plus reported strong second-quarter results, driven by robust software performance and growth in the ALI and MC business units. While services revenue faced delays, the growing backlog and reaffirmed FY 2025 guidance underscore management’s confidence. Key priorities include leveraging cross-selling opportunities and achieving historical EBITDA margin targets, with strategic focus on navigating the challenging macroeconomic environment.

Read the full Earnings Call Transcript

data.title: Natgas immune to tariff turmoil so far, but LNG looks like bargaining chip in future trade deals
data.description:
U.S. natural gas bucked the sharp losses suffered by other commodities on Thursday, as the Trump administration's tariffs and their feared impact on global growth sent crude and other markets into a tailspin.

While natural gas looks somewhat insulated in the immediate term from the broader economic worries, "the larger consideration within the next 12 months is if macroeconomic disturbance... will weigh on near-term industrial and power sector demand to the extent that it derails a bullish structural setup for natural gas into winter 2025-26," according to Eli Rubin of EBW Analytics.

The biggest question, Rubin added, is how long the tariff framework stays in place, as they give President Trump "massive leverage" in trade negotiations.

"The weather is usually the driver of gas pricing and this week's cooldown that appears poised to extend into next week is currently offering support" for natgas prices, Ritterbusch analysts wrote.

The Energy Information Administration reported a 29B cf storage build for last week, a third straight increase which was within the range of expectations, and the early start to the injection season, which typically begins in April, has reduced the deficit over the year-ago and five-year average levels, Dow Jones reported.

"We expect a deficit to remain intact throughout this month and any consensus updates suggestive of a hot summer could have an exaggerated price impact," Ritterbusch said.

Front-month Nymex natural gas (NG1:COM) for May delivery closed +2% to $4.138/MMBtu, its best settlement in two weeks; U.S. natgas has gained 13.9% YTD and 133% from 52 weeks ago.

ETFs: (NYSEARCA:UNG), (BOIL), (KOLD), (UNL), (FCG)

Regardless of the new tariffs, the U.S. gas market looks to be affected as exports of gas in the form of LNG look set to become a bargaining chip in any ensuing trade maneuvers, according to Gavin Maguire at Reuters.

For countries seeking to trim their trade surplus with the U.S. or avoid being hit with future tariffs, commitments to scale up purchases of U.S. liquefied natural gas are an effective means of quickly rebalancing the trade ledger in favor of the U.S., but at the same time, countries affected by new tariffs that are already regular buyers of U.S. LNG may threaten to cut those purchases as part of possible reprisals, Maguire wrote.

"That means that in any event, the U.S. gas market looks set to be buffeted by the impending trade turbulence, with gas exporters, utilities, households and businesses all likely to be impacted by the upcoming swings in gas trade volumes and prices."
